name: Spaghetti Squash with Mushrooms and Shrimp
recipeInstructions:
1. Boil the shrimp with seasonings, let cool, then peel and devein. Cut the shrimp into small pieces or shred. Set aside.
2. Weigh the spaghetti squash. Calculate microwave time as 6 minutes per pound. Place the whole squash on a glass pie plate. Microwave on high for 2 minutes; pierce the rind in 4-6 places with an ice pick. Flip halfway through cooking and microwave on high for the remaining time.
3. Cut the squash in half, remove seeds. Use a fork to scrape strands from the rind. Place the strands in a 1 1/2 qt casserole dish.
4. In a 2-cup glass measure, place mushrooms and cover with vented plastic wrap. Microwave on high for 1 3/4 minutes. Remove mushrooms and add 1 tablespoon margarine to the liquid.
5. Blend flour into the liquid with a wire whisk, then add milk. Whisk halfway through cooking and microwave on high for 2 minutes, or until thickened. Stir in sherry, grated parmesan cheese, and pepper.
6. Add cooked mushrooms and shrimp to the squash strands. Mix well.
7. Melt remaining 1 tablespoon margarine in a custard cup by microwaving on high for 30 seconds. Blend in corn flake crumbs. Distribute over the top of the squash.
8. Microwave on high for 1 minute, or until hot.
